subject: [PATCH v5 02/11] mm: add PMD swap entry splitting support

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch adds support for splitting a PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries in the Linux kernel's memory management (mm) module. The patch introduces a new branch in the __split_huge_pmd_locked() function that handles PMD swap entries, which are used to represent large blocks of swap space. Unlike migration splits, no folio reference is needed for swap entries because they point to swap slots rather than pages. The patch also includes changes to handle the case where a present THP split has already cleared the pmd, and adds warnings to catch any future callers that break an invariant related to the freeze path.
